conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
I felt most connected to I, E, and D. With I we locked into a 50/50 partnership, a two-tap signal, and a clear plan to stick together each round, which felt solid and dependable. E and I shared a similar approach — cooperate early, compare notes, and adapt — and E’s plan to meet at the whiteboard after round one showed strategic transparency I liked. D was upfront, favored a small verifiable test (cooperate round 1 and check in), and matched my preference for promises backed by follow-up.

I felt least connected to J — our chat was polite but high-level and left me with little concrete information. Yes: I made explicit agreements (cooperate round 1 with D; cooperate first two rounds and share observations with E; a 50/50 split and two‑tap signal with I). I’m going into the game trusting I, E, and D more than others; I don’t yet trust J (not enough rapport) and will watch anyone who’s loud, inconsistent, or makes big promises.
